name: ship-crew-quarters
summary: A low-ceilinged berth where the crew swings in ranks of hammocks — sea chests under every hook, the air thick with sweat and tar.

primary_palette.note: paired ranks of slung hammocks — typical crew berth
a table rigged down for meals, stows between watches
communal kit stow for the watch

story_prompts: off-watch sleeping — an argument brewing in the corner
recent fight — one hammock cut down, blood on the planks
empty, mid-battle — the crew is all topside
plague has taken half the watch, shrouds wrapped in the hammocks
anti_patterns: hammocks don't touch the floor — they hang from beams; leave clear floor beneath
don't scatter chests randomly — each hammock has its chest directly under it
no elegant furniture — everything is rough, functional, salt-bleached
